completed_tasks.name: Multi-user data isolation — users/sessions tables, session_id cookie, all queries scoped by user_id
Fix DB migration — drop legacy account_id tables, recreate with user_id INTEGER FK
Fix auth redirect — new users go to / not /no-project (route not in React Router)
Add try/except + logging to OAuth callback DB block — catches all exceptions, logs traceback, redirects to /?error=token_exchange_failed instead of raw 500

remaining_tasks.name: Restart container + account 2 tries login + check docker logs for logged exception
Fix root cause of account 2 login failure (TBD — depends on logged exception)
Verify login works end-to-end for BOTH accounts — clear cookies → OAuth → land on ConnectionPage with no error
Verify sync — add Jira project → sync completes without 'Sync failed'
UAT: two browser profiles, different Atlassian accounts → confirm isolated project lists
UAT: Bug Report Word (.docx) and Sprint Word (.docx) exports
Responsive UI polish UAT: sidebar animations, card layouts, navbar logo

blockers.description: Account 2 login still fails with raw 500 — exact error unknown until container restart + fresh attempt + log check
blockers.workaround: auth.py now has try/except that logs the traceback. Restart container, account 2 logs in, check docker logs immediately for ERROR line.

human_actions_pending.action: Commit auth.py: git add backend/routers/auth.py && git commit -m 'fix: catch and log all exceptions in OAuth callback DB block'
human_actions_pending.context: auth.py has uncommitted try/except + logging addition. Must commit before container restart.

human_actions_pending.action: Restart backend container: powershell.exe -Command "docker restart group2_warnnoi300percent-backend-1"
human_actions_pending.context: uvicorn --reload is blind to NTFS changes from WSL. Manual restart required.

human_actions_pending.action: Account 2 attempts login, then immediately run: powershell.exe -Command "docker logs group2_warnnoi300percent-backend-1 --tail 30 2>&1" — look for ERROR line with traceback
human_actions_pending.context: auth.py now logs the exception. The logged traceback will reveal the root cause of the 500.

decisions.decision: Proper users+sessions tables with INTEGER user_id FK on all data tables
decisions.rationale: Codebase was already refactored to this model. database.py migration now drops+recreates legacy account_id tables on startup.

decisions.decision: session_id cookie (opaque token) not jira_session (account_id value)
decisions.rationale: Proper session abstraction. session_id → user_id lookup in sessions table. Safer than storing account_id directly in cookie.
/no-project not in React Router — triggers *-catch-all. Direct to / (ConnectionPage) instead.

decisions.decision: Wrap auth callback DB block in try/except with logger.error(traceback) + redirect to /?error=token_exchange_failed
decisions.rationale: Account 2 login was returning raw 500. Error was not visible in logs. try/except ensures: (a) traceback logged for diagnosis, (b) user gets friendly error page, (c) no more raw 500.

next_action: 1. git add backend/routers/auth.py && commit. 2. docker restart backend. 3. Account 2 logs in. 4. Check docker logs immediately for 'OAuth callback DB/session error' line. 5. Fix root cause.
context_notes: DB schema confirmed correct (user_id=True, account_id=False on all 4 data tables). Only 1 user in DB (ps@manaosoftware.com, id=1). The 500 errors in old logs were pre-fix. But account 2 is STILL getting raw 500 on callback. Root cause unknown — could be FERNET_KEY issue, FK constraint, or something else. The new try/except in auth.py will expose the real error in docker logs. Important: 500 errors in log grep were from same Atlassian account_id as account 1 — so those failures were account 1 before the migration fix, NOT account 2. Account 2 has never successfully logged in.
